Elements.RawText: - If the reader is invalid, IReaderStatus::getValid returns false.

Lines.FullText: - If an event packet was encountered during processing, IReaderStatus::isEventEncountered returns true.

Elements.RawText: - If an event packet was encountered during processing, IReaderStatus::isEventEncountered returns true.

Lines.FullText: - If the reading process is successful, ReaderStatus::isOk returns true, indicating that IReaderStatus::getValid is true and IReaderStatus::isEventEncountered is false.

Elements.RawText: - If the reading process is successful, ReaderStatus::isOk returns true, indicating that IReaderStatus::getValid is true and IReaderStatus::isEventEncountered is false.

Tags.RawText:
@param[out] status: Represents the status of the reader.
     * - If the reader is invalid, IReaderStatus::getValid returns false.
     * - If an event packet was encountered during processing, IReaderStatus::isEventEncountered returns true.
     * - If the reading process is successful, ReaderStatus::isOk returns true, indicating that IReaderStatus::getValid is true and IReaderStatus::isEventEncountered is false.

Lines.FullText: A signal data reader that abstracts away reading of signal packets by keeping an

Elements.RawText: A signal data reader that abstracts away reading of signal packets by keeping an

Lines.FullText: internal read-position and automatically advances it on subsequent reads. The difference to

Elements.RawText: internal read-position and automatically advances it on subsequent reads. The difference to

Lines.FullText: a StreamReader is that instead of reading on per sample basis it always returns only a full block of samples.

Elements.RawText: a StreamReader is that instead of reading on per sample basis it always returns only a full block of samples.

Lines.FullText: This means that even if more samples are available they will not be read until there is enough of them to fill

Elements.RawText: This means that even if more samples are available they will not be read until there is enough of them to fill

Lines.FullText: at least one block.

Elements.RawText: at least one block.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief A signal data reader that abstracts away reading of signal packets by keeping an
 * internal read-position and automatically advances it on subsequent reads. The difference to
 * a StreamReader is that instead of reading on per sample basis it always returns only a full block of samples.
 * This means that even if more samples are available they will not be read until there is enough of them to fill
 * at least one block.

Lines.FullText: Currently only supports single-dimensional scalar sample-types and RangeInt64
